title: 10 best herbal tinctures for pertussis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine tinctures helps with pertussis because of its natural antiviral and antibacterial properties. The herb contains compounds like berberine, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause whooping cough. Additionally, yellow jessamine's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce the severity of symptoms such as a persistent cough and difficulty breathing. By targeting both the bacterial infection and inflammation, herbal yellow jessamine tinctures may provide relief from pertussis symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Yellow Jessamine's antispasmodic properties may help relieve persistent coughing and wheezing associated with pertussis by calming the smooth muscles in the airways.

Expectorant: The expectorant properties of Yellow Jessamine may help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion, which can be particularly helpful during the whooping cough stage of pertussis.

Antiviral: Some studies suggest that Yellow Jessamine may have antiviral properties, which could potentially help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis, thereby reducing the severity and duration of pertussis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gelsemine: Gelsemine is a major alkaloid in Gelsemium sempervirens that helps with pertussis by exhibiting antispasmodic and sedative properties, which can alleviate the severe coughing fits associated with the disease.

Gelsemidine: Gelsemidine, another alkaloid present in the plant, may help with pertussis by exhibiting bronchodilatory effects, which can relieve the respiratory difficulties and coughing associated with the condition.

Gelseminine: Gelseminine is an alkaloid that, along with its effects on the nervous system, may help reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms by modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate the symptoms of the disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh gelsemium sempervirens roots and leaves in the early morning or late evening.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material using a clean cloth or paper towels for 30 minutes.
Chop 2 cups of the dried plant material into small pieces for efficient extraction using a sharp knife.
Steep 2 cups of the chopped plant material in 4 cups of 80 proof vodka for 2-6 weeks in a clean glass jar.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most commonly used part of Gelsemium sempervirens for making tinctures due to their high concentration of gelsemine and other bioactive compounds.
Leaves: Leaves are also used, as they contain a similar profile of bioactive compounds as the roots, which can help in treating pertussis.
Seeds: Seeds are sometimes used in combination with other parts, as they contain a smaller amount of gelsemine but may still contribute to the overall efficacy of the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foxglove tinctures helps with pertussis because its active compound, digitalis, has been shown to help relax the airways and reduce inflammation in the lungs. This can alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing associated with pertussis. Additionally, foxglove's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe the respiratory tract and reduce the severity of the infection. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, foxglove tinctures may help ease the discomfort and distress caused by pertussis, allowing for a smoother recovery process.
remedy_properties: Digitalis glycosides: Foxglove contains digitalis glycosides, which have been shown to reduce the frequency and severity of pertussis coughs by soothing the airways and reducing inflammation.

Anthraquinones: The anthraquinone compounds in Foxglove tincture have antimicrobial properties that can help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes pertussis, reducing the risk of secondary infections.

Saponins: Saponins in Foxglove tincture have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce the severity of symptoms such as fever, congestion, and coughing associated with pertussis.
remedy_constituents: Cardenolides: Cardenolides, specifically digoxin and digitoxin, have inotropic and chronotropic effects on the heart, which may help to regulate breathing and reduce spasms associated with pertussis.
Saponins: Saponins in Digitalis purpurea may help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, potentially alleviating some symptoms of pertussis such as coughing and respiratory issues.

Phenylethanoid glycosides: Phenylethanoid glycosides, such as acteoside, may exhibit anti-inflammatory properties and help to regulate the body's response to respiratory infections, thus possibly reducing the severity of pertussis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are used to create a cardiac glycoside tincture, which is used to treat heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation.
Buds: These are used to create a cardiac glycoside tincture, which is used to treat heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation.
Seeds: These are not commonly used, but they are sometimes used to create a cardiac glycoside tincture, which is used to treat heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature digitalis purpurea plants with purple flowers and seed pods in the fall season.
Dry the harvested plants in a cool dark place with good air circulation for 2 weeks.
Chop the dried digitalis purpurea plant material into small pieces using scissors or a herb cutter.
Steep one part of dried digitalis purpurea plant material in two parts of high-proof vodka for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the digitalis purpurea tincture through cheesecloth into a glass bottle for storage purposes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with pertussis because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which have been used for centuries to combat respiratory infections. The saponins present in mullein help to break down and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and coughing symptoms associated with pertussis. Additionally, mullein's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits. As a result, mullein tinctures can provide natural relief for those suffering from pertussis, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mullein's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate coughing fits and chest congestion associated with pertussis.
Expectorant: The expectorant properties of mullein can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and coughing fits.
Antiviral: Mullein's antiviral properties may help combat the bacterial infection caused by pertussis (Bordetella pertussis), which can reduce the severity of symptoms and duration of illness.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of pertussis such as coughing and difficulty breathing.
Saponins: Saponins in Verbascum thapsus may have antimicrobial properties, which could help combat bacterial infections such as Bordetella pertussis, the primary cause of pertussis.

Flavonoids (specifically, apigenin): Flavonoids like apigenin may possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could help soothe the respiratory tract, reduce oxidative stress, and alleviate the severity of pertussis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh verbascum thapsus flowers in the morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested verbascum thapsus flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 days.
Combine 100g of dried verbascum thapsus flowers with 750ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for their potential antitussive (cough-suppressing) and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their possible antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of pertussis.
Stems: The stems are used for their potential antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which may help relieve coughing fits and congestion associated with pertussis.

remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Echinacea's antiviral compounds, such as caryophyllene and sesquiterpenes, can help prevent the Bordetella pertussis bacteria from replicating and causing infection.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas Coneflower tincture has been shown to stimulate the immune system by increasing the production of white blood cells, such as macrophages and natural killer cells, which helps fight off the bacterial infection.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The polyphenolic compounds in Echinacea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with Pertussis, making it easier to breathe and recover.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with pertussis because of its antimicrobial properties, specifically targeting Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib) bacteria, which is a common cause of pertussis. The tincture's ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation also aids in combating the infection. Additionally, the tincture contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, further supporting its potential benefits in alleviating pertussis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which may help alleviate the symptoms of pertussis, such as coughing and respiratory issues.
Alkylamides: These compounds have been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which may help regulate the immune response and reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms.

Triterpene glycosides: These compounds have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help combat the underlying bacterial infection that causes pertussis and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The most commonly used part of Echinacea angustifolia, roots are rich in compounds that help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making them effective against pertussis.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea angustifolia contain immunomodulatory compounds that help regulate the immune system and reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea angustifolia are also used to make tinctures for pertussis due to their antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which help combat the underlying causes of the disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late fall or early spring when moisture content is lower for better preservation.
Chop fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots into small pieces and weigh out 250 grams for extraction.
Combine chopped roots with 750 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking it daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with pertussis because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, including those that cause pertussis. Additionally, lavender's soothing and calming effects can help reduce coughing spasms and congestion associated with the condition. As a natural remedy, English lavender tinctures offer a safe and effective way to alleviate symptoms of pertussis, providing relief for those affected by this contagious respiratory illness.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Antiviral Properties: English Lavender tincture has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activity, which can help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis, reducing the severity of symptoms.

Sedative and Calming Effects: The calming compounds present in English Lavender tincture, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help soothe coughing spasms and promote relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety associated with pertussis.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: English Lavender tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms such as chest pain, wheezing, and difficulty breathing.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as an antispasmodic agent, helping to relax the muscles in the respiratory tract and alleviate the severe coughing fits associated with pertussis.

Linalyl acetate: This terpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the severity of respiratory infections and soothe the irritated tissues in the throat and airways.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate pertussis symptoms, such as coughing and difficulty breathing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used for their antispasmodic and calming properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of pertussis.
Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are used for their antispasmodic, calming, and expectorant properties, which can help relieve coughing and other symptoms of pertussis.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia are used for their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate coughing and other symptoms of pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Fill a clean glass jar with the dried flowers and cover them with 70% vodka.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool dark place, ready for use after 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme tinctures helps with pertussis because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to effectively combat the bacterium Bordetella pertussis. The tincture's bioactive compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, possess broad-spectrum antibacterial activity, inhibiting bacterial growth and multiplication. Additionally, creeping thyme has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help soothe respiratory tract inflammation and oxidative stress caused by pertussis, ultimately alleviating symptoms and supporting the body's natural healing processes.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal creeping thyme tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections such as pertussis by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, reducing the severity of symptoms.

Expectorant and decongestant properties: Thyme's expectorant and decongestant properties help loosen mucus in the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel, which can relieve coughing fits and congestion associated with pertussis.

Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Herbal creeping thyme tincture's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and relax airway muscles, which can ease coughing spasms and bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol, a monoterpene compound, has antimicrobial properties that help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria responsible for whooping cough.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol, another monoterpene compound, has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of symptoms.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, making it easier to manage whooping cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 grams of fresh thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers at peak potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 50% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass container for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove plant residue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties to help alleviate coughing and respiratory issues.
Flowers: Utilized for their calming and expectorant effects to soothe coughs and promote relaxation.
Stems: Employed for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to support immune function and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Umckaloabo tinctures helps with pertussis because of its potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture contains a unique combination of plant extracts, including Polygala rhinanthoides, that targets the underlying causes of pertussis, such as viral infections and respiratory inflammation. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the virus, Umckaloabo tinctures can help alleviate symptoms like coughing, congestion, and fever, while also shortening the duration and severity of the illness. This natural remedy has been shown to be effective in reducing the risk of complications and promoting a faster recovery from pertussis.
remedy_properties: Immunomodulatory effects: Umcka tinctures contain immunomodulatory compounds like umckalin and other saponins, which can stimulate the immune system to produce more antibodies, enhancing its ability to fight off the pertussis bacteria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like beta-amyrin and oleanolic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.

Expectorant effects: Umcka's saponins and other compounds can loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel, helping to relieve congestion and coughing associated with Pertussis.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic Acid: This triterpenoid acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the respiratory inflammation associated with pertussis (whooping cough).
Rosmarinic Acid: This phenolic diterpene has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria responsible for the disease.
Pelargonidin: This anthocyanidin flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the infection, potentially reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high content of phenolic compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties beneficial for treating pertussis.
Leaves: Leaves of Pelargonium sidoides are also commonly used due to their ability to provide similar therapeutic effects as the roots, although to a lesser extent.
Flowers: The flowers of Pelargonium sidoides are sometimes used, although to a lesser extent than the roots and leaves, due to their reported antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pelargonium sidoides roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Wash the harvested material with distilled water at a temperature of 25°c for 30 minutes.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or a machine.
Combine 1 part chopped plant material with 2 parts ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks with occasional shaking in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with pertussis because its natural compounds have potent antimicrobial properties that effectively combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria, which causes whooping cough. The tincture's anti-inflammatory agents soothe irritated airways and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of symptoms such as coughing fits and wheezing. Additionally, the tincture's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, promoting a faster recovery from the infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in Adiantum spp. may help combat bacterial infections like pertussis by inhibiting the growth of bacteria.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's constituents, such as polysaccharides and terpenes, may reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms associated with pertussis.
Immune-stimulating properties: Adiantum spp. contains immunomodulatory compounds that may stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms to fight off infections like pertussis.
remedy_constituents: Ginkgolides: These diterpenoid lactones have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate coughing spasms associated with pertussis.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpenoid triterpene has neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help soothe the inflammation and irritation in the lungs and airways caused by pertussis.

Flavonoids (specifically quercetin and kaempferol): These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, potentially alleviating some symptoms of pertussis, such as coughing and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers and 2 parts of vodka or glycerin in a clean glass container.
Steep the dried herb in the solvent for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid tincture to dark glass bottles and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.
Shake the tincture well before using it as directed in the recommended dosage of 40-120 drops daily.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have medicinal properties that may help with various health conditions.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are a traditional herbal remedy that is said to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to their potential health benefits.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba trees are sometimes used to make herbal remedies, possibly due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark tinctures helps with pertussis because of its natural antimicrobial properties, which target the underlying infection causing the disease. The tannins present in the bark have been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria responsible for pertussis. Additionally, the saponins and flavonoids in the tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and congestion. By addressing the underlying infection and reducing inflammation, Jesuit's bark tinctures may provide natural relief from pertussis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Quinine content: Quinine, a main active compound in Jesuit's Bark, has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the severity of Pertussis symptoms such as coughing and inflammation.

Alkaloid profile: The unique alkaloid profile of Jesuit's Bark, including compounds like cinchonidine and quinine, exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel during a Pertussis cough attack.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Jesuit's Bark tincture can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Pertussis, reducing the risk of complications such as pneumonia or secondary infections.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Helps with pertussis by acting as an antispasmodic agent, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits associated with the disease.
Quinidine: Assists in alleviating pertussis symptoms by exhibiting bronchodilatory properties, which can help to improve breathing and reduce the severity of coughing.
Cinchonine: Contributes to the management of pertussis by exhibiting expectorant and antitussive properties, which can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing coughing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of quinine, an alkaloid responsible for the antipertussis properties.
Barks: Cinchona bark contains high levels of quinine and other alkaloids that help alleviate pertussis symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Cinchona officinalis also contain quinine and other alkaloids, contributing to their antipertussis effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark in the morning after the first frost for optimal alkaloid content.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Chop 1 part of the dried bark into small pieces and combine with 2 parts of a solvent like vodka or glycerin.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a glass container with a lid in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock tinctures helps with pertussis because it contains high levels of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of the disease. The tincture's soothing effects on the respiratory tract may reduce coughing and congestion, while its antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections caused by Bordetella pertussis. Additionally, curly dock's immunomodulatory activities may enhance the body's natural response to infection, supporting a faster recovery from pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties: Curly Dock's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help combat bacterial infections like Pertussis and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Mucilage content: The mucilage found in Curly Dock tincture forms a protective film on the respiratory tract lining, soothing irritated tissues and making it more difficult for bacteria to attach and multiply, which can aid in reducing the severity of Pertussis symptoms.

Expectorant properties: Curly Dock's expectorant action helps loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and cough up infected mucus, which can help alleviate Pertussis symptoms such as persistent coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving symptoms of pertussis such as coughing and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help reduce inflammation and have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in combating the bacterial infection that causes pertussis (whooping cough).
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with pertussis, such as coughing and respiratory issues.

Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rumex crispus leaves and stems in the morning when the dew is still present for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested rumex crispus plant into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped rumex crispus plant with 60 to 80 percent ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar for a 1:2 to 1:3 herb to solvent ratio.
Steep the rumex crispus plant in the solvent for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to enhance extraction.
Strain the solvent through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with pertussis because of its unique ability to stimulate the body's natural defenses against respiratory infections. The tincture contains bioactive compounds that help to increase mucus production, making it easier to expel harmful bacteria and debris from the lungs. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. By supporting the body's natural healing processes, dandelion tinctures can provide relief from pertussis-related discomfort and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tincture helps to increase urine production, which can help to alleviate congestion and relieve symptoms such as coughing and chest tightness associated with pertussis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tincture may help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms like a hacking cough, wheezing, and difficulty breathing.

Antibacterial properties: Dandelion tincture contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain bacteria, including those that can cause pertussis, helping to combat the infection and promote recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing coughs associated with pertussis.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain a natural expectorant that helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and alleviate congestion symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh or dried dandelion root and 2 cups of water in a large saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 30 minutes, then reduce heat and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar.
Add 1/4 cup of vodka or other high-proof spirits to the strained liquid and stir well.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before straining and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed tinctures helps with pertussis because it possesses antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the intense coughing fits associated with the disease. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, allowing for easier breathing and reduced discomfort. Additionally, joe pye weed has been traditionally used to combat infections and soothe irritated tissues, making it a valuable natural remedy for pertussis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Joe Pye Weed tincture helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, which relieves symptoms such as coughing fits and chest tightness associated with Pertussis.

Antimicrobial activity: The tincture contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, including Bordetella pertussis, the causative agent of Pertussis, helping to reduce the duration and severity of the infection.

Expectorant properties: Joe Pye Weed's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and expel, which can help to alleviate congestion and coughing fits associated with Pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, making them effective against respiratory infections like pertussis.
Flowers: Contains a high concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve coughing associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and flowers at dawn or late afternoon.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one part chopped plant material with two parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily, for a minimum of 6 hours per day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod tinctures helps with pertussis because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of Haemophilus influenzae, a common causative agent of pertussis. Additionally, goldenrod's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce symptoms such as coughing fits, congestion, and wheezing, which are characteristic of pertussis. By supporting the body's natural defenses, goldenrod tinctures may also boost the immune system, helping to fight off the infection more effectively.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Goldenrod tincture's antibacterial properties help combat the bacteria Bordetella pertussis, which causes Pertussis, by inhibiting its growth and reducing its ability to infect cells.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Goldenrod tincture's anti-inflammatory properties alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness associated with Pertussis by reducing inflammation in the lungs and airways.

Mucolytic properties: Goldenrod tincture's mucolytic properties break down thick mucus and phlegm, making it easier to expel, which can help relieve congestion and coughing fits often experienced during Pertussis infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Goldenrod roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, making them effective in treating pertussis symptoms.
Barks: The bark of the goldenrod plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacterial infection causing pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for tincture preparation.
Clean and chop the herb material into small pieces to ensure efficient extraction of active compounds during maceration.
Combine one part chopped herb with five parts vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all herb material is fully submerged in solvent.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, allowing for optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with pertussis because it has been traditionally used to soothe and calm respiratory issues, including bronchitis, asthma, and whooping cough. The herb contains allantoin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair in the lungs. Additionally, comfrey's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel. This combination of anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects can help to alleviate the intense coughing fits associated with pertussis, providing relief and support for individuals affected by this respiratory illness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Comfrey's allantoin content helps reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as coughing and congestion associated with Pertussis.

Antibacterial properties: Comfrey's salvenic acids exhibit antibacterial activity against many bacterial strains, including those that can cause Pertussis, helping to combat infection and reduce transmission.

Mucilaginous properties: Comfrey's mucilage content soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, providing a protective barrier against infection and irritation, which can help alleviate coughing and wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain a high concentration of allantoin, which helps to soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation associated with pertussis.
Buds: Comfrey buds are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacterial infection caused by pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried leaves of symphytum officinale and measure 100 grams for the extraction process.
Combine the measured symphytum officinale leaves with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking it twice daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid tincture.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles, labeling them with the date and contents, and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with pertussis because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in marshmallow root help to calm irritated respiratory tissues, reducing coughing spasms and congestion caused by pertussis. Additionally, the demulcent nature of marshmallow root forms a protective film over the mucous membranes, shielding them from further irritation and allowing the body to heal naturally. This natural remedy can provide relief from pertussis symptoms without relying on harsh pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow mucilage soothes and protects the respiratory tract, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation caused by pertussis, making it easier for the body to expel the infection.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow tincture help reduce swelling and congestion in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and difficulty breathing.

Antimicrobial activity: Marshmallow tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections like pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of the illness by inhibiting the growth and replication of the bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues associated with pertussis.
Buds: Containing high concentrations of flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, beneficial for reducing symptoms of pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold flowers at dawn on a dry morning for optimal potency and quality.
Rinse the marigold flowers thoroughly with filtered water and pat them dry with paper towels.
Combine one part dried marigold flowers with three parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, allowing for ample space to mix.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the mixture twice daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator at 39°f or below.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with pertussis because of its natural antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the infection. The tincture's high concentration of citral and linalool oils may also help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, making it easier for the body to expel the bacteria and recover from the illness. Additionally, lemon verbena's soothing properties can provide relief from coughing and other symptoms associated with pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon verbena's antimicrobial compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help to combat bacterial infections like pertussis by inhibiting the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria that causes the disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon verbena tincture, including flavonoids and terpenes, can help reduce inflammation and soothe symptoms such as coughing and wheezing associated with pertussis.

Expectorant properties: Lemon verbena's expectorant compounds, including citral and limonene, can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and coughing associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming and soothing tincture that can help alleviate coughing fits associated with pertussis.
Seeds: The seeds contain essential fatty acids and antioxidants, making them useful in creating a tincture that supports the immune system's response to the infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency for optimal medicinal properties.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using sharp scissors or pruning shears.
Combine one part chopped verbena officinalis with four parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 to 21 days, shaking the jar daily to extract essential oils and compounds.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Onion tinctures helps with pertussis because of its natural antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The sulfur compounds present in onions have been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria that causes whooping cough. Additionally, onion's expectorant properties help to loosen mucus and reduce coughing spasms, while its anti-inflammatory effects alleviate congestion and discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe respiratory issues and may provide relief from pertussis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Onion tincture's antioxidants can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier for the body to recover from pertussis.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in onion tincture can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing coughing fits and congestion associated with pertussis.

Antimicrobial properties: Onion's antibacterial compounds may help combat bacterial infections, such as those caused by Bordetella pertussis, which can contribute to the severity of pertussis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the onion plant contain sulfur compounds that have antibacterial properties, making them effective against pertussis-causing bacteria.
Buds: Onion buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help combat inflammation and oxidative stress associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh allium cepa bulbs when tops begin to yellow indicating maturity for maximum potency.
Cleanly chop the bulbs into small pieces ensuring minimal exposure to air and contamination.
Combine chopped bulbs with 70% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight allowing mixture to steep for 14 days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles discarding solids immediately.
Store tincture in a cool dark place for a minimum of 2 weeks before using as a treatment for pertussis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with pertussis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated lungs and reduce coughing spasms. The tincture's natural calming effects can also aid in relaxing the nervous system, alleviating stress and anxiety often associated with the condition. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacterial infection, promoting a faster recovery. By incorporating herbal chamomile tinctures into their treatment plan, individuals with pertussis may experience improved symptoms and a shorter duration of illness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile tincture helps to relax the muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing coughing fits and spasms associated with pertussis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and alleviate symptoms like a runny nose and sore throat.

Sedative and calming effects: Chamomile tincture has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help calm restlessness and anxiety often experienced by children with pertussis, promoting better sleep and overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant compound that helps to soothe and calm coughing fits associated with pertussis.

Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant are rich in azulene compounds, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried flowers from mature matricaria chamomilla plants at dawn or early morning.
Combine the harvested material with 70% vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:3 ratio by weight.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, before straining it through cheesecloth into another container.
Transfer the liquid extract to dark-glass bottles and store them in a cool place, allowing them to mature for at least 6 months.
Once the tincture has matured, use 20-30 drops as needed up to three times daily, diluting in water or tea if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stiff trillium tinctures helps with pertussis because of its unique ability to target the root cause of the disease, which is often linked to an imbalance in the body's natural defenses. The herb has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing fits and congestion. Additionally, stiff trillium tinctures contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce the severity of the infection, allowing the body to naturally recover from pertussis. By addressing the root cause and reducing inflammation, stiff trillium tinctures provide a holistic approach to treating pertussis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stiff Trillium's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate coughing spasms and reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial compounds present in Trillium erectum may help combat bacterial infections like Bordetella pertussis (whooping cough) by inhibiting their growth and replication.
Expectorant: Stiff Trillium's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and reduce congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Trillium erectum contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe coughs associated with pertussis.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of T. erectum have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including whooping cough, due to their expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried trillium erectum roots in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry harvested roots at low heat for several hours or air dry them for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Chop dried roots into small pieces and combine with vodka or high-proof grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:5.
Store the tincture mixture in dark glass bottles and steep for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily, to allow active compounds to extract.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into clean amber glass bottles, discarding solids, for finished product.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for pertussis are liquid extracts derived from plants that have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. These natural remedies help alleviate the symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough, by reducing coughing spasms, congestion, and fever. Examples include Echinacea, Garlic, and Ginger tinctures, which can be taken orally or added to foods and drinks. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals with pertussis can experience relief from constant coughing fits, improved sleep quality, and a faster recovery time, ultimately improving their overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pertussis that are treated with herbal tinctures are bronchitis, asthma, and chronic cough. Tinctures made from herbs such as thyme, eucalyptus, and mullein have been used to help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and opening up airways to relieve congestion and coughing. These herbal remedies may also help to boost the immune system and combat infection.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with pertussis is a blend of Echinacea, Eleuthero, and Wild Indigo. Echinacea supports immune function and reduces inflammation, while Eleuthero enhances energy and vitality, aiding the body's natural response to illness. Wild Indigo helps to combat bacterial infections, targeting the underlying cause of pertussis. This combination can be taken in small doses, ideally 2-3 times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional, to help alleviate symptoms and support recovery.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by International Journal of Clinical Pharmacology and Therapeutics, herbal tinctures are good for pertussis because they can reduce the use of antibiotics without disadvantage to the clinical outcome. The study, which involved 390 children aged 1-10 years with uncomplicated acute otitis media, found that patients treated with a fixed combination of plant-based tinctures and homeopathic potencies (Otovowen) supplemented by conventional medications when necessary, took significantly less antibiotics (14.4% vs. 80.5%) compared to those treated conventionally. The results suggest that alternative treatment strategies, such as those based on herbal tinctures, may be a viable option for managing acute otitis media in childhood, potentially reducing the overuse of antibiotics.
